goal: Replace the rejected synthetic UI with a real crypto-card discovery, comparison, and analytics foundation.
completed: Built the rights-aware pipeline: safe CryptoAgg AST discovery for 42 programs, documented PaymentScan API-only adapter, quarantined database import, and ADR 0003 publication boundaries.
Shipped the editorial-fintech catalog, 42 profiles, comparison, and custody/network/card-model analytics with explicit discovery and licensing states.
Applied approved migrations 20260717193000_real_source_ingestion and 20260717194000_candidate_evidence; all three repository migrations are current.
Implemented fail-closed official collection from 11 allowlisted MetaMask, ether.fi, and Gnosis Pay sources, including a narrow public Zendesk API fallback.
Persisted 24 scoped observations across 11 official artifacts: MetaMask 8, ether.fi 7, and Gnosis Pay 9; all three candidates are VERIFYING and global PROMOTED remains zero.

Caught and repaired volatile raw-markup artifact identity during the required rerun: removed 5 duplicate artifacts only after equivalence/reference guards passed, switched to normalized visible-text hashes, and confirmed stable 24-evidence/11-artifact counts after another collection.
Replaced the MetaMask, ether.fi, and Gnosis Pay competitor avatars with pinned official issuer assets carrying source, dimensions, observation date, rights basis, and SHA-256 provenance.
Added a checked public projection that shows the 24 official observations as review-pending evidence on the three priority profiles without labeling fields verified or promoting candidates.

Deployed protected secret-free preview dpl_8oqTbTFrms3PupiCSUufcH18vuqG at https://cardstats-991wxilo9-aterli.vercel.app; authenticated checks confirm the priority profiles and pinned asset return 200.

Removed the discovery-observation notice and repeated Unverified/verification-warning labels across catalog, home, comparison, and profiles; replaced all native selects, including the private research form, with a shared keyboard-accessible custom listbox.
Deployed protected preview dpl_9KcpwS4vvCqMarD4SqrSjfSeeich at https://cardstats-hku2smbz1-aterli.vercel.app; authenticated catalog, MetaMask profile, and comparison smoke checks return 200.
nextActions: Independently review the 24 official observations, normalize regional offering boundaries, and promote only supported fields through the publication workflow.
Select and verify the next official-source program batch, including official media provenance.
Configure a licensed PaymentScan API key and republication-rights flag only if those rights have actually been obtained.
decisions: The synthetic Atlas program remains only as a database/publication test fixture; its public route redirects to the real catalog.
CryptoAgg supplies private discovery observations, not verified claims, copied prose, rankings, layout, or fake card art.
PaymentScan data enters only through its documented paid API; public display additionally requires documented republication rights.
The request plane reads a checked projection and never triggers source collection.
Broad discovery coverage and official verification are displayed as separate counts and states.
Catalog-distribution analytics are program counts, never transaction market share; address metrics are never called users.
The public design uses one editorial research system rather than user-category tiles, bento cards, glassmorphism, or crypto terminal styling.
Official observations may be shown as review-pending evidence, but verified labels, ranking inputs, and promotion remain gated by independent review.
Discovery provenance remains in headings, field language, and the evidence model; repeated per-component warning labels are omitted to keep the public interface legible.
openQuestions: Which programs should follow MetaMask, ether.fi, and Gnosis Pay in official-source verification if priority is not inferred from traffic and geographic breadth?
Has CardStats obtained PaymentScan API access and public republication rights?
When will the exposed development database password be rotated and the initial credential-bearing Vercel deployment be explicitly authorized for deletion?

passed: ESLint, strict TypeScript, Prisma validation/generation, and Git whitespace checks pass.
All three migrations are applied and the database publication integration workflow passes.
Database discovery import remains one run with 42 DISCOVERED and zero PROMOTED candidates.
Database official collection: 24 evidence rows, 11 artifacts, 11 sources, three VERIFYING candidates, and zero PROMOTED after guarded duplicate repair and corrected rerun.
Normalized content hash stability: two consecutive read-only fetches produced identical hashes for all 11 sources.
Official media invariant: three priority assets match their pinned SHA-256 hashes and allowlisted official source hosts.
Final database probe: 24 evidence rows, 11 artifacts, zero range-dash display values, and zero PROMOTED candidates.
npm test: 8 files and 20 tests passed after official media, public-projection, and selector UI coverage.
The 49-page production build passes. Playwright passed all 8 desktop/mobile axe, custom-selector, responsive, reduced-motion, text-scale, landscape, and touch checks.
The updated desktop catalog was visually inspected against the CardStats design system after removing repeated warning labels and introducing the custom selector.
Vercel preview dpl_9KcpwS4vvCqMarD4SqrSjfSeeich is Ready; catalog, MetaMask profile, and comparison return authenticated HTTP 200.
notRun: PaymentScan live API: no API key or public republication-rights confirmation is configured.
Manual VoiceOver and NVDA: unavailable in this environment; semantic and axe checks passed.
Vercel production promotion: only a protected preview was deployed; production alias was not changed.

risks: The 42-record public projection contains minimal competitor-discovered facts and must remain described as reported observations rather than verified terms until official-source review; competitor taxonomies may flatten regional offerings or be stale.
The remaining 39 issuer avatars still resolve through CryptoAgg-observed unavatar URLs and must stay discovery-only until replaced with official media provenance.
The 42 database candidates remain competitor-discovered and unverified; none may be promoted without independent official evidence and review.
PaymentScan analytics cannot be publicly reproduced without a paid API agreement and republication rights.
The development database credential disclosed earlier remains a rotation risk, and the initial credential-bearing Vercel deployment remains live without explicit deletion authorization.
Official help pages can change without notice; collection therefore fails closed when curated evidence phrases disappear and still requires independent review before promotion.
doNotRepeat: Do not print, stage, log, copy, or send the disclosed database credential to another provider.
Do not present discovery observations as verified, rank on them, or silently normalize missing values to zero.
Do not call PaymentScan private frontend server functions, bypass authentication, or republish paid data without rights.
Do not invent card artwork or reuse CryptoAgg faux cards, prose, ranking, layout, or identifiers.
Do not deploy the functional /research workbench until authentication and authorization controls exist.
